attempts.insight: Dual-flank analogue of the project keystone conjugateEquiv_restrictFunctorComp_inv, generalised away from the restrict world. `← conjugateEquiv_comp` SPLITS a conjugate-of-composite (simp lemma only combines). Must use `Functor.whiskerRight/Left` (bare names shadowed to monoidal whiskering by `open MonoidalCategory` → MonoidalCategoryStruct failure). Composite-immersion αhf needs EXPLICIT `naturality := (h≫f).appIso_inv_naturality i` (aesop default fails for h≫f).

next_steps: (a) closed sorry-free + axiom-clean. Feeds c.2, but does NOT slot in directly (see c.2 milestone).

findings.blocker: Two-immersion interleaved merge: the H1 factors and the sliceDualTransport factors are INTERLEAVED in the c.2 goal; (a) does not slot in without sliding the sDT factors past the H1 factors via the single-immersion crux. Mirrors the ~600-line proven tensor pullbackTensorMap_restrict (hslide/comp_cancel_mid).

attempts.insight: c.2 is NOT '(a) then (b)' as two independent factor-identities. (a) closes independently; the sliceDualTransport flank (b) is ENTANGLED with the merge and only becomes well-posed once the slide is fixed. Reconciling the middle-leg functor mismatch requires sliding sDT factors past H1 via per-open crux presheafDual_pullback_restrict_natural (CLOSED iter-066).

next_steps: Re-spec: do NOT keep (b) standalone. Prove c.2 by (i) rw inverted (a) for H1's, (ii) slide each sliceDualTransport factor with presheafDual_pullback_restrict_natural, mirroring pullbackTensorMap_restrict's hslide/comp_cancel_mid. (b) should be the SPECIFIC slide identity step (ii) needs, in the goal's exact spelling, NOT an abstract sDT composition law.
